response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

A 310-line billing module with 5 top-level functions. The two named by findings are:

- **`generateInvoice`** (lines 50–253): the workhorse — 204 lines mixing what looks like invoice assembly, plan/refund logic, and time-stamped behaviour (7 wall-clock reads at lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273).
- **`calculateRefundPreview`** (lines 40–44): named like a pure calculation but actually calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` — it mutates and sends mail.

It carries an unencoded policy comment at line 39: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."* The `"enterprise"` plan literal is hard-coded at line 7. Four TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers between lines 183–308.

## Related files to read before editing

**Direct collaborators of `calculateRefundPreview` (must read):**
- whichever file defines `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` — not in the scan output, grep for them.

**Shared `"enterprise"` literal (will drift if you change behaviour here):**
- `src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`

**Shared temporal pattern (likely the right place for a clock abstraction):**
- `src/date.ts` — same Temporal Recklessness finding, 5 more `Date.now()`/`new Date()` calls.

**Tests that won't catch your regressions:**
- `src/billing.test.ts` — one test has zero assertions ("renders the enterprise plan"), another uses only weak matchers ("creates an invoice"). Don't trust them as a safety net.

**Concept-alias context (if you touch tenant/account terminology):**
- `docs/billing.md`, `docs/teams.md`, plus `src/routes/{account/subscription,team/index,workspace/members}.tsx`.

**Route/nav consistency (if you touch billing UI surface):**
- `src/routes/settings/billing.tsx`, `src/nav/registry.ts`, `src/nav/sidebar.ts` — the `/settings/billing` destination has 4 competing labels (Plans, Billing, Subscription, PricingPage).

## Risks the scan flagged on this file

| Severity | Charge | Where | What it means for your edit |
|---|---|---|---|
| **high** | God Function | `generateInvoice` 50–253 | Editing one section risks unseen interactions elsewhere in the body. Extract before changing behaviour. |
| medium | Temporal Recklessness | 7 sites | Any behaviour you add that depends on time will be untestable without monkey-patching. |
| medium | String Sprinkles | `"enterprise"` line 7 | Changing the plan model here leaves 3 other files stale. |
| medium | False Identity | `calculateRefundPreview` | Callers may treat it as pure — adding more side effects compounds the trap; consider splitting pure preview from the audit/email side. |
| medium | God File | 310 lines | Above the 300-line threshold; small edits collide with code an agent didn't load. |
| medium | Logic in the Alibi | line 39 comment | The owner/enterprise/billing-support rule lives only in prose. If your edit touches refund auth, encode this rule in code before extending it. |
| low | Unfinished Business | 4 markers, 183–308 | Hard to tell which nearby behaviour is intentional vs. flagged-not-final. |

One finding (`crime_00003`) was suppressed (`suppressed_count: 1`) — worth a `crimes context` with `--include-suppressed` if your edit is non-trivial.
